---
title: '$\Lambda$CDM Cosmological Model'
nocite: |
  @Carmona:2022pro
---

\section{Field equations}

In order to describe an homogeneous and isotropic universe like in the framework of GR, one should solve the Einstein field equations using a homogeneous, isotropic, and expanding metric (the FLRW metric),
\begin{equation}
    ds^2 = dt^2 - a^2(t) \left(\frac{dr^2}{1-k r^2}+r^2 d\Omega\right) \,,
    \label{eq:FLRW}
\end{equation}
where $k$ is the curvature and $a(t)$ the scale factor of the universe, which is closely related to the redshift, $a(t)=1/(1+z(t))$. We can use this relation to also connect the temporal coordinate to the redshift variable,
\begin{align}
    a(t) &=\frac{1}{1+z(t)} \\ 
    \frac{\dot a(t)}{a(t)} dt &= -\frac{dz}{1+z(t)} \\
    dt &= -\frac{dz}{H(z)(1+z)} \,,
    \label{eq:dtdz}
\end{align}
where we have defined the Hubble parameter[^1] as $H=\dot a/a$.

[^1]: We will not discuss here the Hubble tension problem.

\section{Friedman equations}

Using the FLRW metric, Eq. \eqref{eq:FLRW}, to solve the Einstein equations leads to the Friedmann equations, which are the basis of the standard cosmology. The first Friedmann equation can be written in terms of the cosmic density of radiation ($\Omega_r$), matter ($\Omega_m$), curvature ($\Omega_k$), and dark energy ($\Omega_\Lambda$),
\begin{gather}
    H(z) = H_0 \sqrt{\Omega_r (1+z)^4 + \Omega_m (1+z)^3 + \Omega_k (1+z)^2 + \Omega_\Lambda} \,.
    \label{eq:Hz_full}
\end{gather}
The model does not predict the values of these cosmic densities, so they should be adjusted using cosmological observations and measurements. For more information see \cite{ReyesHung:2023udr}.

\section{Mermaid map}

:::{#mindmap-container}
```{mermaid}
mindmap
  root(("<a href='index.html'>Wiki Home</a>"))
    math_node("<a href='doc/Math/index.html'>Mathematics</a>")
      calc("<a href='#'>Calculus</a>")
      linalg("<a href='#'>Linear Algebra</a>")
      stats("<a href='#'>Statistics & Probability</a>")
      topology("<a href='#'>Topology</a>")
      algebra("<a href='#'>Abstract Algebra</a>")
    phys_node("<a href='doc/Physics/index.html'>Physics</a>")
      cm("<a href='#'>Classical Mechanics</a>")
      qm("<a href='#'>Quantum Mechanics</a>")
        seq("<a href='#'>Schrödinger Equation</a>")
        dirac("<a href='#'>Dirac Equation</a>")
      thermo("<a href='#'>Thermodynamics</a>")
      em("<a href='#'>Electromagnetism</a>")
    cs_node("<a href='#'>Computer Science</a>")
      algo("<a href='#'>Algorithms</a>")
      ds("<a href='#'>Data Structures</a>")
      ai("<a href='#'>Artificial Intelligence</a>")
        ml("<a href='#'>Machine Learning</a>")
        nn("<a href='#'>Neural Networks</a>")
      os("<a href='#'>Operating Systems</a>")
    bio_node("<a href='#'>Biology</a>")
      gen("<a href='#'>Genetics</a>")
      evo("<a href='#'>Evolutionary Biology</a>")
      cell("<a href='#'>Cellular Biology</a>")
```
:::
    
<style>
  #mindmap-container {
    width: 100%;
    height: 600px;
    border: 1px solid #ccc;
    overflow: hidden;
    background: #fff;
    position: relative;
  }
  /* This prevents the SVG from squishing or stretching */
  #mindmap-container svg {
    width: 100% !important;
    height: 100% !important;
    max-width: none !important;
  }
</style>

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/svg-pan-zoom@3.6.1/dist/svg-pan-zoom.min.js"></script>

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
  var timer = setInterval(function() {
    var svg = document.querySelector('#mindmap-container svg');
    if (svg) {
      clearInterval(timer);

      // Clean the attributes that cause the "sinking" and blurring
      svg.removeAttribute("width");
      svg.removeAttribute("height");

      var panZoom = svgPanZoom(svg, {
        zoomEnabled: true,
        controlIconsEnabled: true,
        fit: true,    // Force it to fit the box initially
        center: true, // Force it to center the actual content
        minZoom: 0.1,
        maxZoom: 20,
        mouseWheelZoomEnabled: true
      });

      // Force a refresh to correct the alignment after Mermaid finishes
      setTimeout(function() {
        panZoom.resize();
        panZoom.fit();
        panZoom.center();
      }, 500);
    }
  }, 200);
});
</script>

## List of Authors {id="authors" .appendix}

- Albert Einstein
- Isaac Newton
